description: Tasks template for modernization plan. Generate this file alongside plan.md to track individual migration tasks.

tasks.taskSummary: Upgraded ContosoUniversity from .NET Framework 4.8 to .NET 10 (net10.0). Converted project to SDK-style, migrated ASP.NET MVC 5 to ASP.NET Core MVC, replaced Global.asax with Program.cs, updated routing, removed System.Web.Optimization, upgraded EF Core to 10.0.8, replaced MSMQ with in-memory queue placeholder, and migrated file upload to IFormFile. Build: 0 errors, 0 warnings.
tasks.description: Upgrade ContosoUniversity from .NET Framework 4.8 to .NET 10 (net10.0), converting the project to SDK-style and migrating all ASP.NET Framework APIs to ASP.NET Core.
tasks.reason: The project targets .NET Framework 4.8 which is end-of-life for mainstream support, uses legacy System.Web APIs incompatible with modern Azure SDKs, and has a non-SDK-style project file that blocks modern tooling. Upgrade is required to run on Azure App Service with .NET 10.
tasks.requirements: Upgrade the project target framework from .NET Framework 4.8 to net10.0. Convert the project file from legacy non-SDK-style to SDK-style format. Migrate ASP.NET MVC 5 (System.Web) to ASP.NET Core MVC. Migrate application startup from Global.asax.cs to Program.cs. Convert RouteCollection route registration to ASP.NET Core endpoint routing. Remove System.Web.Optimization bundling and minification; replace with direct HTML references to static files. Remove or replace all incompatible NuGet packages. Update all NuGet package references to versions compatible with net10.0. Resolve binding redirect conflicts. Addresses assessment issues: Project file needs to be converted to SDK-style, Project's target framework(s) needs to be changed, NuGet package is incompatible, NuGet package functionality is included with framework reference, Binary incompatible for selected .NET version, Source incompatible for selected .NET version, ASP.NET Framework (System.Web), Routes registration via RouteCollection is not supported in .NET Core, Convert application initialization code from Global.asax.cs to .NET Core, System.Web.Optimization bundling and minification is not supported in .NET Core, MSMQ & Message Queuing (framework-level), Legacy Configuration System, Binding redirect forces version downgrade, NuGet package upgrade is recommended, NuGet package is deprecated, Static content detected.

tasks.taskSummary: Migrated MSMQ NotificationService to Azure Service Bus with Managed Identity (DefaultAzureCredential). Added INotificationService interface, async methods, JSON serialization, unit tests (18/18 passed). Build: 0 errors, 0 warnings.
tasks.description: Migrate the MSMQ-based NotificationService from System.Messaging to Azure Service Bus with Managed Identity authentication.
tasks.reason: MSMQ (System.Messaging) is a Windows-only, on-premises messaging technology that is not supported on Azure App Service or .NET 10. Azure Service Bus is the cloud-native replacement that provides reliable asynchronous messaging with Managed Identity support.
tasks.requirements: Replace all System.Messaging usage in Services/NotificationService.cs with Azure Service Bus SDK. Use Managed Identity (DefaultAzureCredential) for authentication to Azure Service Bus — no connection strings or credentials. Maintain the same send/receive notification semantics currently provided by the MessageQueue. Update the queue path configuration to use the Azure Service Bus namespace and queue name. Addresses assessment issues: MSMQ usage is detected, Convert System.Messaging to MSMQ in .NET Core, Binary incompatible for selected .NET version (NotificationService.cs).

tasks.taskSummary: Migrated SQL Server LocalDB connection to Azure SQL Database with Managed Identity (Authentication=Active Directory Default). Updated appsettings.json and Web.config, converted SchoolContextFactory to IDesignTimeDbContextFactory, added 11 new unit tests. Build: 0 errors, 29/29 tests passed.
tasks.description: Migrate the SQL Server database connection from a hardcoded LocalDB connection string to Azure SQL Database using Managed Identity (passwordless) authentication.
tasks.reason: The application uses a hardcoded SQL Server LocalDB connection string in Web.config with Integrated Security, which is not suitable for Azure deployment. Azure SQL Database with Managed Identity provides secure, passwordless access without storing credentials.
tasks.requirements: Update the Entity Framework Core SQL Server provider configuration to target Azure SQL Database. Replace the hardcoded LocalDB connection string with Azure SQL Database connection using Managed Identity (DefaultAzureCredential / passwordless). Remove the Integrated Security connection string from Web.config / appsettings.json. Ensure EF Core migrations continue to work with Azure SQL Database. Addresses assessment issues: SQL database connection detected, Connection strings without configuration builders detected.

tasks.taskSummary: Migrated local file system storage to Azure Storage Blob with Managed Identity (DefaultAzureCredential). Added IBlobStorageService interface, AzureBlobStorageService implementation, updated CoursesController, Program.cs and appsettings.json. 20 new tests. Build: 0 errors, 53/53 tests passed.
tasks.description: Migrate teaching material image uploads in CoursesController from local file system storage to Azure Storage Blob with Managed Identity authentication.
tasks.reason: Azure App Service does not provide persistent local file system storage. The current implementation in CoursesController uses Server.MapPath and System.IO to save/read/delete files in ~/Uploads/TeachingMaterials/, which will not work reliably in a cloud environment. Azure Storage Blob provides scalable, durable, and cloud-native file storage.
tasks.requirements: Replace all local file I/O operations in Controllers/CoursesController.cs (file upload, directory creation, file deletion, path resolution via Server.MapPath) with Azure Storage Blob SDK operations. Use Managed Identity (DefaultAzureCredential) for authentication to Azure Storage — no connection strings or access keys. Maintain the same upload, retrieve, and delete file semantics. Update any image path references stored in the database to use Blob Storage URLs. Addresses assessment issues: Local or network IO operations detected.

tasks.taskSummary: Replaced Windows Authentication with Microsoft Entra ID using Microsoft.Identity.Web. Added OIDC configuration in appsettings.json, updated Program.cs with AddMicrosoftIdentityWebAppAuthentication, updated BaseController to use Entra ID claims, added sign-in/out UI. Build: 0 errors, 53/53 tests passed.
tasks.description: Replace Windows Authentication with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) for user authentication, enabling cloud-compatible identity management on Azure App Service.
tasks.reason: Windows Authentication is a Windows domain-only authentication method that does not work in Azure App Service environments. Microsoft Entra ID provides modern, standards-based authentication (OIDC/OAuth2) that is fully supported on Azure.
tasks.requirements: Remove Windows Authentication configuration from Web.config / IIS Express settings. Integrate Microsoft Entra ID authentication using ASP.NET Core authentication middleware. Configure the application to use Entra ID (OIDC) for user sign-in. Update any authentication/authorization code that relies on Windows identity. Addresses assessment issues: Windows authentication detected.

tasks.taskSummary: Externalized non-secret application settings to Azure App Configuration. Added Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.AzureAppConfiguration 8.0.0 package, wired conditional AddAzureAppConfiguration in Program.cs using DefaultAzureCredential (Managed Identity), and emitted .azure/configuration-migration.json seed file with 10 non-secret key values (AzureAd, AzureServiceBus, Storage settings). Secrets and connection strings excluded. Build: 0 errors, 53/53 tests passed.
tasks.description: Externalize non-secret application settings from Web.config / appsettings.json to Azure App Configuration, eliminating hardcoded configuration in the application.
tasks.reason: The application has connection strings and app settings stored directly in Web.config without using configuration builders, making it unsuitable for cloud deployment. Azure App Configuration provides centralized, dynamic configuration management for cloud applications.
tasks.requirements: Move non-secret application settings (such as queue paths, feature flags, and app-level settings) from appsettings.json to Azure App Configuration. Ensure the existing IConfiguration / IOptions<T> shape is preserved so no application code changes are needed beyond configuration registration. Emit a .azure/configuration-migration.json seed file for the deployment agent. Do not move secrets (connection strings, keys) to App Configuration — those belong in Key Vault via managed identity (already handled by SQL and Service Bus tasks). Addresses assessment issues: Connection strings without configuration builders detected.

tasks.description: Scan all project dependencies for known CVEs and remediate any identified vulnerabilities to ensure the application is secure before deployment.
tasks.reason: The assessment identified NuGet packages with known security vulnerabilities and deprecated packages. Remediating these before deployment is required to ensure the application meets security standards.
tasks.requirements: Upgrade vulnerable dependencies to the minimum patched version. If a CVE fix requires a major version upgrade, document the affected dependency, the current version, the upgraded major version, and the breaking change risk. Verify that the project builds and all tests pass after remediation. Addresses assessment issues: NuGet package contains security vulnerability, NuGet package is deprecated.

tasks.description: Generate Bicep IaC files to provision all required Azure resources for the ContosoUniversity application.
tasks.reason: The user explicitly requested Bicep files for Azure deployment. Infrastructure as Code is required to provision Azure App Service, Azure SQL Database, Azure Service Bus, Azure Storage Account, and Azure App Configuration in a repeatable and version-controlled manner.
tasks.requirements: Generate Bicep IaC files to provision the following Azure resources: Azure App Service Plan and Web App (.NET 10), Azure SQL Database (with managed identity access), Azure Service Bus namespace and queue, Azure Storage Account and Blob container for teaching materials, Azure App Configuration store. Configure Managed Identity assignments between App Service and all dependent Azure services (SQL, Service Bus, Storage, App Configuration). Use Azure naming conventions and parameterize environment-specific values (environment name, location, resource names).

tasks.description: Deploy the modernized ContosoUniversity application to Azure App Service using Azure CLI with Bicep-provisioned infrastructure.
tasks.reason: The user requested deployment to Azure App Service. This task packages and deploys the .NET 10 application to the provisioned Azure App Service instance.
tasks.requirements: Build and deploy the .NET 10 ContosoUniversity application to Azure App Service. Use the Bicep infrastructure provisioned in task 008. Configure App Service application settings to connect to Azure SQL Database, Azure Service Bus, Azure Storage, and Azure App Configuration using Managed Identity. Set the .NET runtime stack to .NET 10.
tasks.resourceStatus: New resource to be created via Bicep IaC in task 008-infrastructure-bicep
